channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran hold separate meetings in Qatar and agree to continue discussions
item.description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators met separately on Wednesday with Qatari and Pakistani mediators, with "positive progress made," and they agreed to continue discussions, host Qatar said.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. and Iranian negotiators met separately on Wednesday with Qatari and Pakistani mediators, with "positive progress made," and they agreed to continue discussions, host Qatar said.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Russian missiles and drones kill 11 and cause damage across Ukraine capital
item.description: The large-scale attack with ballistic and cruise missiles and drones damaged buildings and civilian infrastructure across the city. Many residents took shelter at metro stations.
item.content:encoded:
The large-scale attack with ballistic and cruise missiles and drones damaged buildings and civilian infrastructure across the city. Many residents took shelter at metro stations.

(Image credit: Danylo Antoniuk)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Untold casualties and humanitarian needs: What to know a week from Venezuela's quakes
item.description: Here's a look at some of the major developments since major back-to-back earthquakes rocked Venezuela on June 24, devastating parts of a country already reeling from crisis after crisis.
item.content:encoded:
Here's a look at some of the major developments since major back-to-back earthquakes rocked Venezuela on June 24, devastating parts of a country already reeling from crisis after crisis.

(Image credit: Ariana Cubillos)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: Funerals held for 14 Pakistani children killed in tutoring center collapse
item.description: Police are investigating whether negligence during construction work caused the collapse in the eastern city of Lahore on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
Police are investigating whether negligence during construction work caused the collapse in the eastern city of Lahore on Tuesday.

(Image credit: K.M. Chaudary)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Greetings from London, where Banksy's flag man is a warning cry
item.description: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
item.content:encoded: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Ni

item.title: Mexico ends a 40-year knockout drought, beats Ecuador to advance in World Cup
item.description: Mexico lost seven consecutive times at that same stage from 1994 to 2018 and didn't advance past the group stage in 2022. Now, the 40-year wait is over.
item.content:encoded:
Mexico lost seven consecutive times at that same stage from 1994 to 2018 and didn't advance past the group stage in 2022. Now, the 40-year wait is over.

(Image credit: Silvia Izquierdo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Belfast violence: What to know about the fascist youth groups known as 'active clubs'
item.description: Racist mob violence in Northern Ireland earlier this month has drawn keen interest from extremist groups and figures in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: Racist mob violence in Northern Ireland earlier this month has drawn keen interest from extremist groups and figures in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: SCOTUS to rule on birthright citizenship. And, U.S. murder rate nears new low
item.description: The Supreme Court is expected to make a long-awaited ruling on birthright citizenship today, on the high court's last day of its term. And, the U.S. murder rate approaches a record low.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court is expected to make a long-awaited ruling on birthright citizenship today, on the high court's last day of its term. And, the U.S. murder rate approaches a record low.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Venezuelans deported from the U.S. were killed hours later in powerful quakes
item.description: On June 24, 146 Venezuelans were deported from Texas to Caracas. Hours later, while the deportees were in a guarded hotel, powerful twin earthquakes struck.
item.content:encoded:
On June 24, 146 Venezuelans were deported from Texas to Caracas. Hours later, while the deportees were in a guarded hotel, powerful twin earthquakes struck.

(Image credit: Matias Delacroix)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Iran war supercharges pivot to renewable energy
item.description: The Iran war and high oil and gas prices have supercharged the adoption of renewables and EVs worldwide. Global investors say these technologies make financial sense and increase energy security.
item.content:encoded: The Iran war and high oil and gas prices have supercharged the adoption of renewables and EVs worldwide. Global investors say these technologies make financial sense and increase energy security.
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon
